title: Tanker report - Week 26 2016
summary:
VLCCs
It has been a week for owners that has flattered to deceive. The upward momentum from last week has eased and rates have largely gone sideways. Charterers have been drip feeding the market with enquiry in their efforts to undermine owners' confidence and it would appear this tactic has worked.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: It has been a week for owners that has flattered to deceive. The upward momentum from last week has eased and rates have largely gone sideways.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: West Africa rates have started to recover, aided by renewed enquiry both in the north and in Med/Black Sea, helping to thin the tonnage list and charterers looking to cover second decade cargoes found themselves with a more restricted choice of tonnage, as rates firmed around two points to WS 70. The Black Sea has similarly benefitted from these improved levels of enquiry and has risen accordingly around six points to settle at WS 81.25 level for 135,000 tonnes.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: The Mediterranean market has been under pressure. Charterers have been picking off tonnage and the weaker sentiment has seen rates soften, losing around 11 points since the start of the week, to settle at around WS 107.5.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: On the Continent, it has been another difficult week for owners whose cause is not helped by the weak Caribs upcoast market where levels have dropped to the low WS 90s. This has led to tonnage ballasting across with the subsequent improved supply of tonnage leading to rates dropping to WS87.5/90 level for 55,000 tonnes from ARA to the US Gulf.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: In the 37,000 tonnes Cont/USAC trade, healthy amounts of enquiry and a tight tonnage list has pushed the market up almost 10 points to settle now at WS 105. In the US Gulf, the 38,000 tonnes back haul market remains weak , although owners have managed to push rates up 2.5 points to around WS 65.
